blocks.content: Not on purpose — she'd been thinking about other things when she left — but she realized it two blocks away and turned back. Ethan buzzed her up without asking why. There was a small, particular awkwardness that came from returning to a moment you'd already left, which was somehow both less and more than the moment itself.
He had already found the jacket. He held it out. She took it. He kissed her again — brief, and entirely without question — and she was back on the street two minutes later.
Lena was walking the other direction, three buildings down. Their eyes met. Lena took a slow look at Cassandra's hair, at whatever her face was doing, and kept walking without a word.
Her phone lit up as she was taking off her shoes.
I'm not going to say anything.
But your hair had paint in it.
He has a very good jaw.
I'm just saying. Objectively. As someone who is not saying anything.
Cassandra set her phone face-down on the nightstand and lay back on her bed.
She looked at her hand, where the blue paint had dried to a faint shadow between her fingers.
